changes: PicoClaw posted another nightly build on 2026-04-11 00:53 UTC, while stable remains v0.2.6.
Hermes Agent still has no new release since v2026.4.8, but its install/docs surface now clearly includes Android / Termux support.
The broader atlas structure is unchanged today: the monitored set, taxonomy, and executive read still hold.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw shipping again matters because the category anchor is still setting product pace, not just mindshare.
IronClaw shipping on the same day reinforces that the privacy and security lane is staying active, not stalling behind the lightweight clones.
PicoClaw's nightly cadence still reinforces the fast-moving edge and low-resource lane.
Hermes broadening the documented install story to Android helps its portable, always-available personal-agent positioning, but remains secondary to the release activity above.
Net: this was a material day-level update, but it does not change the stable strategic thesis yet.
